---
title: "Diagrams & Clinical Algorithms"
subtitle: "Mermaid and Graphviz — rendered natively, no images to maintain"
---

Quarto renders **Mermaid** and **Graphviz (DOT)** from plain text. Edit the logic,
re-render, and the diagram updates — ideal for versioned clinical algorithms.

## Mermaid: microcytic anaemia work-up

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-microcytic
%%| fig-cap: "Simplified diagnostic pathway for microcytic anaemia."
flowchart TD
    A[Microcytic anaemia<br/>MCV &lt; 80 fL] --> B{Ferritin}
    B -->|Low| C[Iron deficiency]
    B -->|Normal / high| D{Investigate further}
    C --> C1[Find the source:<br/>GI loss, diet, malabsorption]
    D --> E[Serum iron / TIBC]
    D --> F[Haemoglobinopathy screen]
    F -->|HbA2 raised| G[β-thalassaemia trait]
    F -->|Normal| H[Anaemia of chronic disease<br/>or sideroblastic]
    style A fill:#B71C1C,color:#fff
    style C fill:#ffd5d5
    style G fill:#ffd5d5
```

## Graphviz: a simplified coagulation pathway

```{dot}
//| label: fig-coag
//| fig-cap: "Convergence of intrinsic and extrinsic pathways on the common pathway."
digraph coag {
  rankdir=TB;
  node [shape=box, style="rounded,filled", fillcolor="#f5f5f5", fontname="Helvetica"];
  edge [color="#7f0000"];

  subgraph cluster_i { label="Intrinsic (APTT)"; color="#B71C1C";
    XII -> XI -> IX -> "IXa/VIIIa"; }
  subgraph cluster_e { label="Extrinsic (PT)"; color="#B71C1C";
    "TF/VIIa"; }

  "IXa/VIIIa" -> Xa;
  "TF/VIIa"  -> Xa;
  Xa [fillcolor="#ffd5d5"];
  Xa -> "Thrombin (IIa)" -> Fibrin;
  "Thrombin (IIa)" [fillcolor="#ffd5d5"];
  Fibrin [fillcolor="#B71C1C", fontcolor="white"];
}
```

::: {.callout-tip}
Because these are text, they diff cleanly in git and never go stale against an
exported PNG.
:::
